claim: Benchmarking is a subset of evaluation: evaluation is comprehensive end-to-end assessment, benchmarking is controlled comparison of specific models/tasks
actionable: When scoping eval projects, distinguish between comprehensive evaluations (covering many aspects) and specific benchmarks (comparing models on fixed tasks)

claim: A pyramid approach to evaluating LLMs: base layer is system performance (throughput, latency, GPU utilization), then formatting/output structure, then factual accuracy, then safety/bias/custom evals
actionable: When building eval suites, prioritize system performance first; without fast/reliable inference, model accuracy is irrelevant

claim: Evals should be incremental: start with component-level evals (e.g., retrieval quality in RAG), then integration evals (component interactions), then end-to-end UI evals
actionable: Don't attempt full system evals immediately; build incrementally from component-level tests upward based on priority

claim: Different system types require different evals: RAG systems need retrieval evals (e.g., Ragas), agents need tool-calling evals, and custom evals are specific to application logic
actionable: When choosing eval frameworks, match the framework to your system type (RAG, agent, fine-tuned, etc.) rather than using a one-size-fits-all approach

claim: GuideLLM is a tool for system performance benchmarking; adjust input/output tokens based on your use case (chatbot vs RAG) to realistic scenarios before deploying
actionable: Use GuideLLM to benchmark inference performance (latency, throughput, P99) with input/output tokens matching your production use case before deployment

claim: lm-eval-harness (with MMLU Pro) enables standard accuracy benchmarking; MMLU Pro is more comprehensive than original MMLU and runs in minutes with harness
actionable: Use lm-eval-harness to run MMLU Pro benchmarks for baseline accuracy assessment; customize the eval with your proprietary datasets by forking the repo

claim: PromptFU (and similar custom eval tools) allow building arbitrary safety/bias/custom evals with full flexibility; these are essential for production AI systems
actionable: For safety and custom evals specific to your domain, use PromptFU or similar frameworks to build domain-specific eval suites beyond generic benchmarks

claim: Evals must be integrated into CI/CD pipelines to track production performance continuously; evaluation is not a one-time check but an ongoing automated process
actionable: Implement CI/CD-integrated eval frameworks that run automatically on model updates and production traffic to catch regressions early

claim: Synthetic data generated by AI models in training sets degrades diversity and precision over generations; accuracy evals help detect and mitigate this model collapse
actionable: Include diversity and precision metrics in your accuracy evals to detect synthetic data collapse; periodically validate training data against real-world human data sources
